explanation: This command uses the common netcat flag '-lvp' which is detected by the Sigma rule. It simulates a listener on port 8080.
This command uses equivalent flags '-l -v -p' instead of '-lvp' to achieve the same behavior. The executable name is also changed to 'nc.exe' as per the Sigma rule.
This command uses the '-lvnp' flag combination, which is another variation detected by the Sigma rule. The executable name is changed to 'ncat.exe'.
This command uses the '-vnl -e' flag combination to execute a shell, which is a common netcat reverse shell pattern detected by the Sigma rule.
This command uses the '-l -v -p' flags and includes the '--proxy-type http' option, which is another variation covered by the Sigma rule.
